title: Stigmaphyllon auriculatum
intro: Stigmaphyllon auriculatum, also known as the "ear-leaved calyptra," is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stigmaphyllon auriculatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Stigmaphyllon auriculatum belongs to the family Malpighiaceae, commonly known as the Barbados or Malpighia family. This family is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, Africa, and Asia. The genus Stigmaphyllon is a part of this family and is primarily found in the Americas. The species Stigmaphyllon auriculatum is a type of flowering plant native to South America. It is a member of the order Malpighiales, which is a group of flowering plants that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions.
